steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content: A radius drawn to a tangent point is perpendicular to the tangent line. The tangent slope is $\frac{5}{4}$, so the radius from the center $(3,7)$ to $(a,-4)$ has slope $-\frac{4}{5}$.

steps.title: Write the slope equation
steps.content: Use the two points on the radius: $$(3,7) \text{ and } (a,-4).$$ The slope is $$\frac{-4-7}{a-3}=\frac{-11}{a-3}.$$ Set this equal to $-\frac{4}{5}$: $$\frac{-11}{a-3}=-\frac{4}{5}.$$

steps.title: Check the perpendicular slopes
steps.content: The radius slope is $-\frac{4}{5}$ and the tangent slope is $\frac{5}{4}$. Their product is $$-\frac{4}{5}\cdot\frac{5}{4}=-1,$$ so they are perpendicular. Choice D is correct.
